description: Pipelined v1 — Job application tracking platform: FastAPI backend, React SPA, MongoDB, Chrome Extension MV3

userStories.title: Initialize monorepo directory structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the monorepo skeleton in place so all subsequent stories have the correct directory layout to write into.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /backend, /frontend, /extension, /shared directories
/backend has __init__.py, /backend/auth, /backend/applications, /backend/calendar, /backend/jobs, /backend/parsing, /backend/middleware, /backend/tests subdirectories
/frontend/src has api/, context/, hooks/, pages/, components/, lib/ subdirectories
/extension has content/boards/, background/, popup/, tests/ subdirectories
.gitignore covers Python (__pycache__, .env, *.pyc), Node (node_modules, dist), and OS artifacts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: FastAPI app skeleton with health endpoint and CORS
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a running FastAPI app with CORS and a health check so deployment infrastructure can verify the service is up.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/main.py exports a create_app() factory function
GET /health returns 200 { status: 'ok' }
CORS middleware allows frontend origin (configurable via ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var)
Lifespan events connect/disconnect the Motor DB client
backend/requirements.txt includes: fastapi, uvicorn[standard], motor, pydantic-settings, pyjwt, bcrypt, httpx, openai, apscheduler, slowapi, structlog
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Motor async client and MongoDB index creation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the async Motor client initialized with proper connection pooling and all required indexes created on startup.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/config.py uses Pydantic BaseSettings; reads MONGO_URI, MONGO_DB_NAME, JWT_SECRET, OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GITHUB_TOKEN from env
backend/database.py has connect(), disconnect(), get_collection(name) functions
Motor client configured with maxPoolSize=50, retryWrites=True, serverSelectionTimeoutMS=5000

ensure_indexes() creates all indexes: users (email unique, google_id sparse unique), applications (user_date compound, duplicate_guard compound unique, user_updated), calendar_events (user_date, app_id), job_listings (url_dedup unique, stale_date, filters)
ensure_indexes() is called in the FastAPI lifespan startup
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auth Pydantic schemas
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the auth request/response Pydantic models so the router and service can reference typed contracts.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/auth/schemas.py defines: RegisterRequest (email, password, display_name), LoginRequest (email, password), GoogleAuthRequest (id_token), UserResponse (id, email, display_name, default_stages), TokenPayload (sub, exp, type)
All request schemas use ConfigDict(strict=True)
UserResponse.from_doc() classmethod maps _id to id as string and excludes password_hash
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auth service: password hashing, JWT, and user CRUD
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the auth business logic layer so routers can delegate JWT creation, password validation, and user creation to a service.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/auth/service.py has: hash_password(plain), verify_password(plain, hashed), create_access_token(user_id), create_refresh_token(user_id), decode_token(token)
bcrypt work factor is 12
Access token TTL is 15 minutes, refresh token TTL is 7 days (from config)
create_user(email, password, display_name) inserts into users collection with default_stages=['Applied','Phone Screen','Onsite','Offer','Rejected']
get_user_by_email(email) and get_user_by_id(user_id) return user doc or None
DuplicateEmailError domain exception defined and raised on duplicate email
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Auth router: register, login, logout, me, refresh
userStories.description: As a user, I want to register, log in, and log out via email/password so I can access my dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/register: creates user, returns 201 + UserResponse, sets httpOnly access + refresh cookies
POST /api/auth/login: validates credentials, returns 200 + UserResponse, sets cookies; returns 401 on wrong password
POST /api/auth/logout: clears both cookies, returns 204
POST /api/auth/refresh: validates refresh token cookie, issues new access token cookie, returns 200
GET /api/auth/me: returns current user profile (requires valid access token)
backend/auth/dependencies.py defines get_current_user() Depends callable that decodes JWT from cookie
HTTPException(401) raised when token is missing or expired
Rate limiting: 5/minute on register and login
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Google OAuth endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sign in with Google so I don't need to create a password.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/google accepts { id_token } in request body
Backend verifies ID token via Google tokeninfo endpoint (GET https://oauth2.googleapis.com/tokeninfo?id_token=...)
If user exists (by google_id or email), retrieves and logs in; otherwise creates new user with google_id set
Returns 200 + UserResponse + sets JWT cookies on success
Returns 401 if Google token verification fails
Uses httpx.AsyncClient for the verification call (no requests library)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Extension token endpoint and rate limiting middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a short-lived extension token endpoint and rate limiting so the extension can authenticate and the API is protected from abuse.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/auth/extension-token requires valid access token cookie, returns { data: { token: '...' } } with a 15-minute JWT
backend/middleware/rate_limit.py configures slowapi: 60/minute standard, 10/minute AI endpoints, 5/minute auth endpoints
Rate limit middleware mounted in main.py
429 response uses the standard error envelope: { error: { code: 'RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED', message: '...' } }
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Docker and docker-compose for local development
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Docker support so I can run the full backend stack locally without manual dependency setup.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/Dockerfile uses multi-stage build: python:3.12-slim, pip install from requirements.txt, non-root user, uvicorn entrypoint
backend/docker-compose.yml defines: app service (FastAPI) and mongo service (mongo:7), with volume mount for hot reload
docker compose up starts the app on port 8000 and MongoDB on port 27017
.env.example file at repo root lists all required env vars with placeholder values
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend skeleton: Vite + React + TailwindCSS + routing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the React app scaffolded with routing and providers so frontend pages can be built on top of a working shell.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/package.json includes: react, react-dom, react-router-dom, @tanstack/react-query, axios, tailwindcss, lucide-react, react-window, date-fns
frontend/src/main.jsx renders App inside BrowserRouter, QueryClientProvider, AuthProvider
frontend/src/App.jsx defines routes: /, /dashboard (protected), /calendar (protected), /jobs, /login, /register
ProtectedRoute wrapper redirects to /login if user is not authenticated
frontend/src/context/AuthContext.jsx exports AuthProvider and useAuth hook; user state is null when not logged in
frontend/tailwind.config.js configured for src/ directory
npm run dev starts Vite dev server; npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Login and Register pages with Google OAuth button
userStories.description: As a user, I want to log in or register via Google or email/password so I can access the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/pages/Login.jsx renders a Google OAuth button and email/password form
frontend/src/pages/Register.jsx renders a Google OAuth button and email/password/name form
frontend/src/api/auth.js exports: register(body), login(body), logout(), getMe(), googleAuth(idToken)
frontend/src/api/client.js: Axios instance with withCredentials: true, response interceptor that retries on 401 via refresh, redirects to /login on refresh failure
Successful login/register updates AuthContext user state and navigates to /dashboard
Form validation shows field-level errors (empty email, short password)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Applications Pydantic schemas
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the application request/response Pydantic models so the router and service have typed contracts.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/applications/schemas.py defines: ApplicationCreate (role_title, company, source, source_url?, compensation?, company_type?, location?, remote_status?, date_applied?, tags?), ApplicationUpdate (all fields Optional), ApplicationResponse (id, role_title, company, current_stage, source, date_applied, updated_at, tags, compensation, location, remote_status, stage_history), ApplicationListQuery (sort_by, sort_order, stage, company_type, remote_status, tags, date_from, date_to, cursor, limit), StatsResponse (total_applied, active_count, response_rate, avg_days_to_first_response)
ApplicationCreate and ApplicationUpdate use ConfigDict(strict=True)
ApplicationResponse.from_doc() maps _id to id as string
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Applications service: CRUD, duplicate guard, stage logic
userStories.description: As a user, I want my application saves to be validated, deduplicated, and to track stage transitions automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/applications/service.py defines: create(user_id, body), list(user_id, query), get(user_id, app_id), update(user_id, app_id, updates), delete(user_id, app_id)
create() checks duplicate via compound index on (user_id, company, role_title); raises DuplicateApplicationError with existing_id if found
create() initializes current_stage='Applied', stage_history=[{stage:'Applied', transitioned_at:now}], stages from user.default_stages
update() with current_stage change appends new entry to stage_history via $push
delete() uses a transaction to also delete linked calendar_events
list() uses cursor-based pagination (no skip()), applies projection for list fields only
All queries include user_id filter
compute_stats(user_id) uses $facet aggregation to return StatsResponse fields in one round trip
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Applications router: all CRUD + stats + stage endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I want API endpoints to create, read, update, and delete applications so the frontend can perform all CRUD operations.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/applications: returns paginated list with meta.next_cursor; all query params from ApplicationListQuery supported
POST /api/applications: returns 201 + ApplicationResponse; maps DuplicateApplicationError to 409 with DUPLICATE_APPLICATION code
GET /api/applications/stats: returns StatsResponse
GET /api/applications/:id: returns full ApplicationResponse including stage_history and linked calendar events
PATCH /api/applications/:id: returns updated ApplicationResponse
DELETE /api/applications/:id: returns 204
POST /api/applications/:id/stages: adds custom stage at given position; returns updated stages array
DELETE /api/applications/:id/stages/:name: removes stage; returns 409 if stage is currently active
All routes require auth via Depends(get_current_user)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Frontend API layer and React Query hooks for applications
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the frontend data layer so components can read and mutate application data through a consistent interface.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/api/applications.js exports: fetchApplications(filters), fetchApplication(id), createApplication(body), updateApplication(id, body), deleteApplication(id), fetchStats()
frontend/src/hooks/useApplications.js exports: useApplications(filters), useApplication(id), useApplicationStats(), useCreateApplication(), useUpdateApplication(), useDeleteApplication()
Mutations invalidate all relevant query keys (KEYS.all, KEYS.stats) on success
Query staleTime: 30s for lists, 60s for stats
useApplications passes filter params as URL query params via URLSearchParams
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: StatsBar and FilterBar components
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my pipeline metrics and filter my applications so I can understand my job search at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: StatsBar.jsx renders 4 metric cards: total applied, active count, response rate (as %), avg days to first response
StatsBar shows skeleton loading state while data loads
FilterBar.jsx renders: stage multi-select, company type multi-select, remote status multi-select, date range picker (date_from, date_to)
FilterBar updates URL search params on change (useSearchParams, replace: true)
FilterBar reads initial state from URL search params on mount
Max 10 Tailwind classes per element
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: ApplicationList component with react-window virtualization
userStories.description: As a user, I want to scroll through all my applications in a performant list so large numbers of applications don't slow down the page.
acceptanceCriteria: ApplicationList.jsx uses FixedSizeList from react-window with itemSize=64
Each row renders: company name, role title, StagePill (colored badge), date applied, source icon
Rows with updated_at > 14 days ago show an amber dot (data-testid='stale-indicator')
Column headers (Company, Role, Stage, Date Applied) are clickable and update sort params in URL
Clicking a row calls onSelect with the application object
STAGE_COLORS constant in lib/constants.js maps stage names to bg/text/dot Tailwind classes
Empty state rendered when no applications match filters
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: DetailPanel component: fields, notes, stage selector, stage history
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open a side panel showing all details for an application and edit fields inline so I can manage each application without leaving the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: DetailPanel.jsx slides in from the right on application select (CSS transform translateX, 250ms ease)
Panel is 480px wide on desktop, full-width on mobile
Closes on Escape keypress or click outside
Displays all ApplicationResponse fields: role_title, company, location, remote_status, compensation, company_type, source_url (link), date_applied
Notes section: textarea that calls useUpdateApplication() on blur
Stage selector: dropdown showing current_stage highlighted; selecting a new stage calls PATCH
Stage history: vertical timeline showing each { stage, transitioned_at } entry in chronological order
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: ManualAddForm modal
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manually add an application without the extension so I can track jobs I applied to outside of supported boards.
acceptanceCriteria: ManualAddForm.jsx is a modal (dialog overlay) triggered by an 'Add Application' button on the Dashboard

Form fields: role_title (required), company (required), source_url (optional), date_applied (date picker, defaults to today), compensation (optional), location (optional), remote_status (select), company_type (select), tags (optional)
Submit calls useCreateApplication(); on 409 DUPLICATE_APPLICATION shows inline error with link to existing application
On success, modal closes and ApplicationList is refreshed via React Query invalidation
source field is hardcoded to 'manual' on submit
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dashboard page composition and sort/filter URL persistence
userStories.description: As a user, I want the dashboard to be a complete, working page where my filter and sort state is preserved in the URL.
acceptanceCriteria: src/pages/Dashboard.jsx composes StatsBar, FilterBar, ApplicationList, DetailPanel, ManualAddForm
Selected application ID stored in URL param ?selected=<id>; DetailPanel opens for that ID on load
Sort params (sort_by, sort_order) stored in URL search params
Filter state from FilterBar passed to useApplications(filters) hook
14-day stale nudge: amber dot visible on ApplicationList rows without interaction needed
Page lazy-loaded via React.lazy in App.jsx
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Chrome extension scaffold and manifest.json
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the extension scaffold in place so board modules and the service worker can be built on top of a valid MV3 structure.
acceptanceCriteria: extension/manifest.json is valid Manifest V3 with: permissions=['storage','activeTab'], host_permissions=['https://api.pipelined.app/*'], content_scripts for LinkedIn/Greenhouse/Lever/Ashby/Workday URLs, service_worker='background/background.js'
extension/content/content.js exists as entry point for content scripts
extension/background/background.js exists as service worker entry
extension/popup/popup.html, popup.js, popup.css exist as stubs
Extension loads without errors in chrome://extensions as an unpacked extension
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: LinkedIn board module
userStories.description: As a user, I want the extension to detect and extract job data from LinkedIn job pages so I can save applications in one click.
acceptanceCriteria: extension/content/boards/linkedin.js exports BOARD_ID='linkedin', isJobPage(), extractFields()
isJobPage() returns true only on URLs matching /linkedin\.com\/jobs\/view\// with the job title selector present
extractFields() returns { role_title, company_name, compensation, company_type, location, remote_status } — any field can be null
Selector chain: primary selector with at least one fallback per field
remote_status derived from keyword scan of page text: 'remote' → 'remote', 'hybrid' → 'hybrid', 'on-site'/'onsite' → 'onsite'
extractFields() never throws — returns nulls for missing data
Tests pass against a saved HTML fixture (tests/fixtures/linkedin-swe-posting.html)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Greenhouse, Lever, and Ashby board modules
userStories.description: As a user, I want the extension to extract job data from Greenhouse, Lever, and Ashby pages so the supported board coverage is complete.
acceptanceCriteria: extension/content/boards/greenhouse.js exports BOARD_ID, isJobPage(), extractFields() — uses .app-title, .company-name, .location selectors
extension/content/boards/lever.js exports BOARD_ID, isJobPage(), extractFields() — uses .posting-headline h2, .posting-categories selectors
extension/content/boards/ashby.js exports BOARD_ID, isJobPage(), extractFields() — uses [data-testid] attributes
All three modules follow the exact same export interface as linkedin.js
All three: never throw, return nulls for missing fields, include fallback selector chains
Tests pass for each module against saved HTML fixtures
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Content script entry, banner injection, and shadow DOM isolation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a non-intrusive banner on job pages so I can save the application with one click without leaving the page.
acceptanceCriteria: extension/content/content.js imports all board modules, finds the matching board, calls extractFields()
If neither role_title nor company_name extracted, no banner is shown
Banner injected as a shadow DOM element (<pipelined-banner>) with mode='closed'
Banner shows: 'Applied for [Role] at [Company]? Save to Pipelined →' CTA button
Banner auto-dismisses after BANNER_AUTO_DISMISS_MS=8000ms with 300ms opacity fade
Escape key dismisses the banner
Banner has ARIA role=alert and aria-live=polite
Z-index 2147483647 on the host element
Banner CSS is injected inline into shadow root (not an external stylesheet)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Service worker: message routing, API calls, and token management
userStories.description: As a user, I want the extension to save my application to the backend when I click the banner so it appears in my dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: extension/background/background.js listens for SAVE_APPLICATION, GET_AUTH_STATUS, GET_RECENT_SAVES message types
handleSave() serializes concurrent saves via a promise queue (saveQueue pattern) to prevent duplicate guard race conditions
fetchWithAuth() reads token from chrome.storage.session, adds Authorization header, retries once on 401 via refreshToken()
On 409 DUPLICATE_APPLICATION response, handleSave() returns { status: 'duplicate', existingId }
On success, caches application to chrome.storage.local recent_saves (max 5 entries)
Auth tokens stored in chrome.storage.session (not local)
Banner receives save result and shows: checkmark (success, dismisses after 1.5s), error with retry (failure), or duplicate message with link
return true in onMessage listener for all async responses
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Workday board module and OpenAI fallback parsing
userStories.description: As a user, I want the extension to save applications from Workday pages even though Workday's DOM is hard to parse directly.
acceptanceCriteria: extension/content/boards/workday.js exports BOARD_ID='workday', isJobPage() for *.myworkday.com/* URLs, extractFields() (returns mostly nulls due to shadow DOM)
When boardId='workday' or both role_title and company_name are null, content.js includes pageText=document.body.innerText (trimmed to 3200 chars) in the SAVE_APPLICATION message
backend/parsing/openai_client.py defines parse_with_openai(page_text) — calls GPT-4o mini at temperature=0.0, max_tokens=200, with strict 6-field JSON system prompt
parse_with_openai() validates response has all 6 keys; returns dict with null values for missing fields
applications/service.py triggers OpenAI fallback in create() when source='extension' and role_title or company_name is missing, using asyncio.gather for parallel operations where possible
If OpenAI call fails, application is saved with whatever partial data exists
Rate limit: 10/minute on the POST /api/applications endpoint (AI endpoint tier)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Extension popup UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open the extension popup and see my recent saves and a link to the dashboard so I can quickly check my pipeline status.
acceptanceCriteria: extension/popup/popup.html has a saves list container, dashboard link, and login prompt section
popup.js sends GET_AUTH_STATUS to service worker on DOMContentLoaded; shows login prompt if not authenticated
Popup reads recent_saves from chrome.storage.local (not from API) and renders up to 5 entries
Each entry shows company name and role title (escaped via textContent before innerHTML injection)
Dashboard link opens https://app.pipelined.app/dashboard in a new tab via chrome.tabs.create
Popup renders in under 200ms (no blocking network calls)
Vanilla JS only — no React or build step
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Calendar backend: schemas, service, and router
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create, view, update, and delete calendar events linked to my applications so I can track interviews.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/calendar/schemas.py defines: EventCreate (application_id, event_type enum, date, time?, notes?, title?), EventUpdate (all Optional), EventResponse (id, application_id, event_type, date, time, notes, title, company, role_title)
backend/calendar/service.py: create_event(), list_events(user_id, date_from, date_to), update_event(), delete_event() — all queries include user_id filter
list_events() joins with applications collection to populate company and role_title on each event
GET /api/calendar/events: accepts date_from and date_to params (default current month); returns events with application summary
POST /api/calendar/events: returns 201 + EventResponse; returns 404 if application_id not found for this user
PATCH /api/calendar/events/:id: returns updated EventResponse
DELETE /api/calendar/events/:id: returns 204
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Frontend calendar API layer, hooks, and CalendarGrid component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a monthly calendar with my interview events so I can track upcoming activities at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/api/calendar.js exports: fetchEvents(dateFrom, dateTo), createEvent(body), updateEvent(id, body), deleteEvent(id)
frontend/src/hooks/useCalendar.js exports: useCalendarEvents(month, year), useCreateEvent(), useUpdateEvent(), useDeleteEvent()
src/components/CalendarGrid.jsx renders a monthly grid — 7 columns, rows for each week
Events appear as colored chips inside day cells, showing company name + event_type
Clicking an event chip opens DetailPanel for the linked application
Clicking an empty day cell opens NewEventForm pre-filled with that date
Month navigation: previous/next arrows and 'Today' button update displayed month
Page lazy-loaded via React.lazy
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: NewEventForm and DetailPanel calendar integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add events from the calendar and see linked events in the application detail panel so everything stays connected.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/NewEventForm.jsx: application selector dropdown (searches by company/role), event_type select, date picker, time input (optional), notes textarea
NewEventForm submits via useCreateEvent(); on success invalidates calendar query and closes form
DetailPanel calendar events section: lists linked events for the current application with event_type, date, time
'Add Event' button inside DetailPanel opens NewEventForm pre-filled with current application_id
Deleting an event from DetailPanel calls useDeleteEvent() and refreshes calendar
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Job listings backend: schemas, service, and router
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse job listings via the API so the frontend can display curated opportunities.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/jobs/schemas.py defines: JobListingResponse (id, company, role, location, remote_status, company_type, experience_level, salary_range, apply_url, date_posted, is_stale, ingested_at), JobListQuery (role_type, experience_level, company_type, remote_status, date_from, salary_min, hide_applied, page, per_page)
GET /api/jobs: returns paginated list (default 30, max 100) with all filters applied; stale listings included but flagged
GET /api/jobs/:id: returns single listing or 404
hide_applied filter: when auth token present, queries user's applications for apply URLs and excludes matching listings
Offset pagination for job listings
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: GitHub job sync scheduler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the APScheduler job to ingest listings from GitHub repos daily so the job board stays fresh.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/jobs/sync.py defines an APScheduler AsyncIOScheduler configured to run at 03:00 UTC daily (configurable via config.github_sync_hour_utc)
Scheduler started/stopped in FastAPI lifespan events
GitHub API client uses httpx with Authorization: token {github_token} header and handles pagination (Link header)
Parser handles SimplifyJobs/Summer2026-Internships README table format: extracts company, role, location, apply_url
url_hash computed as SHA-256 of lowercase stripped apply_url
Each listing upserted via update_one with upsert=True using url_hash as dedup key
Listings with date_posted older than STALE_LISTING_DAYS=60 updated to is_stale=True
Sync errors logged via structlog; scheduler continues even if one sync fails
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: JobCard, JobRow components and JobBoard page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse job listings in either a card grid or compact list view so I can find and apply to new opportunities.
acceptanceCriteria: src/api/jobs.js exports fetchListings(filters)
src/hooks/useJobs.js exports useJobListings(filters) with staleTime=5min
JobCard.jsx renders: company name (initial avatar fallback), role, location, remote_status tag, experience_level tag, 'Apply' button that opens apply_url in new tab
JobRow.jsx renders same data in compact single-line format
Stale listings (is_stale=true) show 'may be closed' label and dimmed opacity
src/pages/JobBoard.jsx: view toggle (card/list) persisted in localStorage; filter sidebar with role_type, experience_level, company_type, remote_status, hide_applied checkboxes
Filters update URL search params
Virtualized list with react-window when in list view
Page lazy-loaded via React.lazy
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Landing page
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see a landing page explaining Pipelined so I understand what it does before signing up.
acceptanceCriteria: src/pages/LandingPage.jsx renders: hero section with product tagline, feature highlights (extension, pipeline view, calendar, job board), CTA buttons for 'Sign Up' and 'Log In', Chrome extension install CTA
Logged-in users visiting / are redirected to /dashboard
Page is fully responsive: desktop (≥1024px), tablet (768–1023px), mobile (<768px)
No API calls on this page
Page lazy-loaded via React.lazy
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Error boundaries and API error surfacing
userStories.description: As a user, I want errors to show readable messages rather than crashes or blank screens so I can understand what went wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: A React ErrorBoundary component wraps each page route in App.jsx
ErrorBoundary renders a user-friendly 'Something went wrong' message with a retry button
API errors from the Axios interceptor surface as { code, message } objects
useCreateApplication() and useUpdateApplication() mutations surface error.message in the UI (inline below form or toast)
404 from GET /api/applications/:id shows 'Application not found' in DetailPanel
No raw stack traces exposed to users in any flow
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Performance optimization: code splitting and direct lucide-react imports
userStories.description: As a developer, I want optimized bundle size and fast page loads so users get sub-second navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 page components lazy-loaded via React.lazy in App.jsx (Dashboard, Calendar, JobBoard, Login, Register, LandingPage)
All lucide-react icon imports use direct subpath format: lucide-react/dist/esm/icons/<name>
All date-fns imports use direct subpath format: date-fns/format, date-fns/startOfMonth, etc.
npm run build completes without warnings about large chunks (no chunk > 500KB)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Accessibility: keyboard navigation and ARIA
userStories.description: As a user with accessibility needs, I want to navigate the application using only a keyboard and a screen reader.
acceptanceCriteria: DetailPanel: focus trapped inside while open; first focusable element receives focus on open
ManualAddForm: focus trapped while open; closes on Escape
All interactive elements (buttons, links, inputs) have visible focus rings
StagePill badges have aria-label describing the stage name
CalendarGrid day cells have aria-label with the date
StatsBar metric cards have aria-label describing each metric
Color is not the only means of conveying the stale indicator (amber dot has aria-label='Stale')
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Security hardening: CORS, CSP, and input sanitization
userStories.description: As a developer, I need production security settings in place so the app is protected before launch.
acceptanceCriteria: CORS allowed origins locked to production domains only (env var ALLOWED_ORIGINS, not wildcard in production)
JWT cookies set with: httpOnly=True, secure=True, samesite='lax'
All Motor queries go through parameterized document filters (no string interpolation into queries)
Extension popup always uses textContent or the escapeHtml function before any innerHTML usage
Backend never logs JWT tokens, passwords, or API keys (verified by searching log calls)
DEBUG=True env var required to enable any dev-only CORS or cookie relaxation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GitHub Actions CI pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CI to run lint, type-check, and tests on every PR so code quality is enforced automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml defines a workflow triggered on pull_request to main
Backend job: checkout, install python 3.12, pip install -r requirements.txt, run pytest tests/ with a test MongoDB URI from secrets
Frontend job: checkout, install node 20, npm ci, npm run build
Extension job: checkout, install node 20, npm ci in extension/, npm test
All jobs must pass for PR to be mergeable (branch protection rule documented in README or CI file comment)
Typecheck passes
